title: poa douglasii var. macrantha
intro: Poa douglasii var. macrantha, also known as Bigelow's bluegrass, is a variety of the Douglas grass (Poa douglasii) that is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various infections. In herbal preparations, Bigelow's bluegrass is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
